subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question situated within a specific biochemical context
Methodological considerations clearly linked to relevant and sufficient data collection
Controlled-variable table with explicit justifications and control methods
Comprehensive materials list with specified tolerances
weaknesses: Methodology description contains ambiguities (unclear symbols, missing sensor calibration and chamber dimensions)
Omissions in controlling pH and oxygen despite acknowledgement
Background section ends mid-sentence, affecting clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (research question context and methodological considerations) clearly met the 5-6 band descriptors, demonstrating specific context and thorough explanations. However, the methodology description fell into the 3-4 band due to ambiguities and omitted instrument calibration and chamber details, and pH/oxygen controls were not fully addressed. Thus, a strong mid-band score of 4 reflects these strengths and shortcomings.

strengths: Data recording and processing communicated clearly in tables and a bar chart
Appropriate statistical analyses performed (averages, standard deviations, ANOVA with Tukey HSD)
Processing of data was largely accurate and relevant to the research question
weaknesses: Precision reduced by misaligned table headings and missing graph labels/legend
Consideration of uncertainties was partial, lacking propagation of sensor or timing errors
Minor calculation inconsistencies and undefined variables in equations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The communication of data was clear, though not fully precise, and statistical processing was largely accurate, matching the 3-4 band for two strands. Consideration of uncertainties showed significant omissions, placing that strand at the lower end of the band. Overall, a score of 4 balances these factors.

strengths: Conclusion relevant to the research question and linked to observed COâ production trends
Some comparison to accepted biochemical context (minerals, antioxidants)
weaknesses: Conclusion not fully consistent with all aspects of the analysis (e.g., control glucose outcome)
Comparison to scientific context remained superficial, lacking quantitative literature support
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands achieved descriptors in the 3-4 band but at the lower end: the conclusion was relevant yet only partially consistent with the analysis, and comparisons to scientific context were brief. A holistic score of 3 reflects this limited consistency and depth.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses described with discussion of their impacts
Realistic improvements proposed and explained in relation to identified limitations
weaknesses: Impact explanations were not deeply quantified
Some suggested improvements (e.g., sugar profiling) lacked full elaboration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation described specific methodological weaknesses and their impacts at the 3-4 band and presented improvements that were well linked to those weaknesses at the 5-6 band. Balancing these performances yields an overall score of 4 for this criterion.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student provides a conclusion that identifies Quercus honey as fastest and relates this to mineral content, while acknowledging data variability.  However, parts of the reasoning (e.g., error-bar interpretation) are only partially consistent with the analysis, and the glucose control outcome is not fully integrated.  Hence the strand fulfils the 3-4 band; a score of 3 reflects limited consistency.

Raw and processed data are laid out in tables and a bar graph with error bars.  Units are mostly present and headings are identifiable, so recording is **clear**; however layout issues (split tables, misaligned headings) reduce precision.  Hence the work meets the âclear OR preciseâ descriptor, placing it in the 3-4 band; a 4 is merited because clarity outweighs the formatting flaws.

Averages, standard deviations and a one-way ANOVA with Tukey HSD are correctly computed, and differences are interpreted.  Minor numerical inconsistencies and a typing error in one sample calculation show **some inaccuracies**, yet overall processing is relevant and largely accurate, matching the 3-4 descriptor; a 4 reflects the generally competent statistical treatment.

Specific weaknessesâpH unmonitored, manual agitation, temperature fluctuation, sugar-ratio assumptionâare **described with discussion of their possible impact** (e.g., enzyme activity, yeast contact).  While the impact analysis is present, it is not deeply quantified, so the explanation level falls short of the highest band, meriting the upper score of the 3-4 band.

Mineral enhancement and antioxidant inhibition are invoked to compare results with accepted biochemical knowledge, demonstrating **some relevant comparison**.  The discussion is brief and lacks citation of quantitative literature values, so it does not reach the depth required for the top band.  This places the work solidly in the 3-4 range at a mid score of 3.

The student states a sharply focused research question and precedes it with a clear, **specific biological context** discussing Saccharomyces fermentative metabolism, honey composition and possible biochemical interactions.  The background explicitly links minerals, antioxidants and complex sugars to enzyme activity and yeast growth, showing the reader why the question matters.  This goes beyond a broad outline and situates the question within a well-defined biochemical framework, satisfying the descriptor for the 5-6 band.

Instrument uncertainties are listed and standard deviation error bars are added, showing **some consideration**.  Nevertheless, there is no propagation of sensor or timing errors and the discussion of overlap is superficial.  These omissions are significant, so the strand sits in the 3-4 band but at the lower score of 3.

Improvements are directly linked to earlier weaknessesâmechanical agitator for mixing, pH monitoring to standardise acidity, more accurate sugar profiling, stable water bathâand each is **explained in terms of how it would enhance reliability or validity**.  The explanations are coherent and realistic; only minor elaboration is missing, so a score of 5 within the top band is awarded.

A detailed table of controlled variables, justification and control method is provided, and assumptions about sugar composition are openly discussed.  The student **explains how each methodological choice (concentration matching, temperature, agitation, sensor timing)** will secure relevant and sufficient data to address the research question.  The explanations go beyond simple statements, so the strand fits securely in the 5-6 range; a top-band score of 5 is awarded due to a few omissions (e.g., pH, oxygen) that are acknowledged only later.

Step-by-step volumes, masses, timings and equipment tolerances are listed, allowing another investigator to reproduce the work with **few ambiguities**.  However, there are some unclear symbols ("q", "baker"), inconsistent units and missing details about sensor calibration and exact chamber dimensions, so the description is not entirely free of omissions.  This aligns with the middle-band (3-4); the stronger end of that band is justified.

annotations.comment: While the conclusion links results to mineral and antioxidant context, quantitative comparison to published literature values would strengthen justification.
Identifying pH monitoring as a methodological weakness is precise; quantify its likely impact on COâ production to prioritize experimental refinements.
The use of a mechanical agitator and pH monitoring are realistic improvements directly addressing mixing inconsistency and acidity variation.
Investigating each honeyâs exact sugar composition prior to experimentation would eliminate assumptions and reduce variability in COâ production data.
The research question is clearly stated within a specific biochemical context, demonstrating strong justification and focus on Saccharomyces fermentative metabolism and honey composition.
The background paragraph ends mid-sentence, creating confusion. Ensure every sentence is complete to maintain reader clarity and context coherence.
Citation formatting varies across footnotes. Adopt a consistent referencing style (e.g., APA or MLA) and include full author, date, title, and URL details.
The assumption that all honey contains exactly 30% glucose and 40% fructose oversimplifies composition. Consider empirical measurement or literature sources for precise sugar profiles.
pH and oxygen are identified as uncontrolled variables but no mitigation is outlined. Introduce pH buffers or oxygen-regulating steps to strengthen reliability.
The hypothesis ranks Quercus honey above glucose/fructose despite noting inhibitory antioxidants. Clarify expected outcomes and reconcile contradictory factors in the prediction.
The methodology lacks details on calibrating weighing boats and volumetric equipment. Include calibration procedures and tolerances for all instruments to ensure reproducibility.
The notation â25qâ is ambiguous. Use standard SI units (e.g., âcmÂ³â) consistently and avoid undefined symbols.
The definition of the measured variable ends abruptly; provide a full description of how COâ production is calculated, including start, end, and baseline measurements.
The controlled-variable table effectively links values to scientific justification and control methods, demonstrating clear and precise methodological considerations.
Consider adding pH regulation within the controlled variables table, as pH fluctuations can significantly affect enzyme activity and COâ production.
Safety precautions are listed but not connected to specific steps; link each precaution to the corresponding protocol to reinforce risk management.
The materials list is comprehensive with specified tolerances, enabling accurate replication of the experiment.
Procedure steps include ambiguous symbols (âÃ·â); replace with clear, descriptive language and consistent formatting for each action.
Qualitative observations are detailed but lack a summary interpretation; add a concise discussion linking these findings to quantitative COâ data.
Table headings are misaligned with their columns, reducing precision. Adjust layout so each header directly corresponds to its data column.
The raw value â20â for Quercus honey in trial 4 is an outlier. Discuss whether this reflects a measurement error or a true experimental result.
The value â9370â in the glucose/fructose trial 5 appears unusually high. Verify data entry and address any anomalies in the analysis.
The average formula uses ârâ without definition. Replace with ân=5â or explicitly define all variables in equations.
Processed data are presented in a run-on paragraph, reducing readability. Format these results into a table or clearly separated list.
The graph lacks axis labels, units, and a legend, making interpretation difficult. Add these elements to communicate data clearly and precisely.
The ANOVA description omits checks for normality and homogeneity of variances. Include these assumption tests to validate the statistical analysis.
The conclusion asserts equivocal findings while also highlighting significance; ensure the narrative fully aligns with the statistical results and error-bar interpretation.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The research question is stated without context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting data relevant to the research question are stated.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data lacks the detail to allow for the investigation to be reproduced.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced with few ambiguities or omissions.
The research question is outlined within a broad context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are described.
The research question is described within a specific and appropriate context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are explained.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The recording and processing of the data is communicated but is neither clear nor precise.
Some processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with major omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.
The recording and processing of data shows limited evidence of the consideration of uncertainties.
The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.
The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.
The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.
The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out appropriately and accurately.
The recording and processing of data shows evidence of an appropriate consideration of uncertainties.
The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Communication of data recording and processing

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The conclusion makes superficial comparison to the accepted scientific context.
A conclusion is stated that is relevant to the research question but is not supported by the analysis presented.
A conclusion is described that makes some relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.
A conclusion is described that is relevant to the research question but is not fully consistent with the analysis presented.
A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.
A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
Realistic improvements to the investigation are stated.
The report states generic methodological weaknesses or limitations.
Realistic improvements to the investigation that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are described.
The report describes specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.
Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
The report explains the relative impact of specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.
